outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Iran Targets Commercial Ships, Dubai Airport as Concerns Grow Over Global Energy — IEA Approves Record Reserve Release
outlets.summary: Reports Iran attacking commercial ships and Dubai airport on Day 12. IEA approves record 400M barrel emergency reserve release. Oil rebounds 3.4% to Brent $90.77 after Monday's crash. Hengaw reports 4,300+ killed — triple government's 1,380 figure.

outlets.name: PBS
outlets.headline: Day 12: Iran Deploys Khoramshahr Super-Heavy Missiles as Lebanon Votes to Ban Hezbollah Military Activities
outlets.summary: Covers Iran deploying its most powerful conventional weapon for the first time. Lebanese cabinet votes 22-2 to declare Hezbollah military activities illegal — historic regional shift. Energy Sec Wright falsely claims gas at $2; AAA data shows $3.578.

outlets.name: MSNBC
outlets.headline: 3 More Ships Attacked at Hormuz as IEA Releases Record 400M Barrels — Only 29% Approve of War
outlets.summary: 3 more ships attacked on Day 12 (14 total damaged/sunk). IEA record 400M barrel release. Oil rebounds +3.4%. Hengaw reports 4,300+ dead. Trump says 'nothing left to target' while Iran launches 37th wave with Khoramshahr missiles.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump: 'Nothing Left to Target' — 90% of Iran's Capability Destroyed. IEA Releases Record 400M Barrels as Oil Rebounds
outlets.summary: Frames Trump's 'nothing left to target' as victory. Reports 90% of Iranian missile launchers eliminated, IEA record reserve release, and oil rebound as markets stabilize. Highlights 16 Iranian minelayers destroyed near Hormuz.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Video Shows US Tomahawk Strike Near Iranian School — Civilian Toll Mounts as Hengaw Reports 4,300+ Dead
outlets.summary: Investigative report on Tomahawk missile striking near an Iranian school. Hengaw human rights organization reports 4,300+ killed — triple Iran government's 1,380 official figure. Growing questions about civilian targeting.

outlets.name: Daily Wire
outlets.headline: Lebanon Makes History: Cabinet Votes 22-2 to Ban Hezbollah Military Activities — Iran's Proxy Network Crumbling
outlets.summary: Frames Lebanese cabinet vote as vindication of pressure campaign against Iran. Argues Iran's proxy network is collapsing. Criticizes Energy Sec Wright's false $2 gas claim as 'own goal' amid war messaging.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran War Day 12: 3 Ships Attacked at Hormuz, Iran Deploys Khoramshahr Super-Heavy Missiles in 37th Wave
outlets.summary: Comprehensive Day 12 coverage: 3 more ships attacked at Hormuz (14 total). Iran deploys Khoramshahr missiles. Lebanese cabinet votes 22-2 against Hezbollah. IEA 400M barrel release. Tehran says 10,000 civilian sites bombed. Trump: 'nothing left to target.'

outlets.name: Reuters
outlets.headline: IEA Approves Record 400M Barrel Emergency Release as 3 More Ships Hit at Hormuz — Oil Rebounds 3.4%
outlets.summary: Reports IEA's unprecedented 400M barrel reserve release. Oil rebounds to Brent $90.77 after Monday's 11% crash. Iran continues shipping oil to China despite blockade. 330+ vessels at anchor. VLCC charter rates at $400K/day.

outlets.name: BBC
outlets.headline: Day 12: Iran Fires Missiles and Drones at Gulf Nations — 3 Ships Hit at Strait of Hormuz
outlets.summary: Reports Iran attacking two vessels in Hormuz after they 'disregarded warnings.' IRGC navy commander says all vessels must get Iran's permission to pass. UK Parliament continues debate on British base usage. 7 US troops killed, 140 wounded.

outlets.name: France 24
outlets.headline: Iran Hits Gulf States in 'Most Intense' Strikes as Lebanon Breaks with Hezbollah — EU Warns of Food Crisis
outlets.summary: Covers Iran's 37th wave targeting Gulf states including Dubai airport and Bahrain. Lebanese cabinet votes 22-2 against Hezbollah. EU warns of cascading food price crisis. Hengaw reports 4,300+ killed.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Iran Deploys Khoramshahr Super-Heavy Missiles — Arrow-3 Intercepts Most. Trump: 'Nothing Left to Target'
outlets.summary: Reports Iran deploying its most powerful conventional missile. Arrow-3 system intercepts multiple Khoramshahr warheads. Growing divergence between Trump's 'nothing left' signals and IDF assessment that Iran retains significant capability.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Lebanon Cabinet Votes 22-2 Against Hezbollah — Israel Sees Historic Regional Realignment
outlets.summary: Frames Lebanese vote as unprecedented shift in regional dynamics. Israel sees Hezbollah's political isolation as a major strategic gain. 14 ships now damaged/sunk at Hormuz. IEA releases record 400M barrels.

outlets.name: Ynet News
outlets.headline: 'They Will Fire Every Missile': Israel Braces for Iran's Khoramshahr as Home Front Fatigue Deepens
outlets.summary: Reports Israel preparing for worst-case scenario as Iran deploys heaviest weapons. IDF says missile launchers reduced from 460 to ~150. Home front fatigue deepening as war enters Day 12. Hengaw death toll: 4,300+.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: IRGC Launches 'Most Intense Operation' — 37th Wave Targets Israel and US Bases. 10,000+ Civilian Sites Bombed
outlets.summary: Frames Iran's 37th wave as the most intense yet. Claims 10,000+ civilian sites bombed by US-Israel. Highlights Khoramshahr super-heavy missile deployment. Vows continued retaliation until aggression stops.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Khoramshahr Super-Heavy Missiles Deployed — Resistance Axis Demonstrates Unbroken Capability Despite Aggression
outlets.summary: Frames Khoramshahr deployment as proof Iran retains full conventional capability. Claims IRGC navy maintaining control of Hormuz. Amplifies Hengaw's 4,300+ death toll to condemn US-Israel targeting of civilians.

outlets.name: Tehran Times
outlets.headline: Lebanon's Vote Against Hezbollah a US-Orchestrated Betrayal — Iran Deploys Most Powerful Missiles
outlets.summary: Condemns Lebanese cabinet vote as American coercion. Frames Khoramshahr deployment as strategic escalation showing Iran will not be deterred. Warns Gulf states that hosting US forces makes them legitimate targets.
